clinical_history: Tall R waves in V1-V2 in a 48-year-old man.  These were due to....
diagnosis: d) Patient had documented prior inferior-posterior myocardial infarction (MI).  Note wide (.04 sec) Q waves in the inferior leads, seen in conjunction with the broad R wave in V1, which is a Q wave equivalent.  Absence of right axis deviation and the presence of upright T waves in V1-V2 are also against right ventricular hypertrophy.
